name: ICICI Prudential Nifty Midcap 150 ETF

index.name: Nifty Midcap 150
index.description: Nifty Midcap 150 captures companies ranked 101-250 by market cap (market cap ₹20K Cr to ₹2 Lakh Cr approximately) — the 'future large-caps' of India's economy. Offers superior 3-4% outper formance vs Nifty 50 over 20+ year periods due to earlier stage growth profiles. Higher volatility but reasonable liquidity enabling retail investment. Ideal for 5-7 year investment horizons where investors tolerate 30-50% drawdowns for 15-20% annual upside capturing. Sector diversity higher than large-caps — includes retail (Trent +2.89%), electronics manufacturing (Dixon +2.34%), emerging realty (Prestige +2.12%), diversified engineering (CG Power, Tube Investments). Captures India's domestic consumption boom through housing, automotive, appliances, and retail. Perfect complement to large-cap SIP — aggressive portion (40% of equity allocation) goes to Nifty 50, remaining 60% to Midcap 150 creating barbell that captures both stability and growth. Rebalance semi-annually provides systematic profit-booking from outperformers (e.g., Trent when Zudio hits 1,000 stores) and reinvestment into laggards (systematic buying low, selling high).

name: Nippon India Nifty SmallCap 250 ETF

index.name: Nifty SmallCap 250 Index
index.description: Tracks the Nifty SmallCap 250 index — stocks ranked 251-500 by market cap. Higher risk, higher potential returns over long horizons. Best suited for SIP investors with 7+ year horizon. Captures India's emerging leaders before they become mid or large cap.

name: Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 100 ETF

index.name: Nifty Midcap 100
index.description: Nifty Midcap 100 captures India's 101st to 200th largest listed companies by free-float market cap — the sweet spot of companies that have proven their business model but still retain significant growth headroom. Historically, Indian midcap indices have outperformed largecaps over 10-year periods owing to higher earnings growth, lower analyst coverage (less efficient pricing), and the 'size premium' rewarding investors for illiquidity risk. MOM100 by Motilal Oswal AMC has been India's go-to passive midcap product since 2011. Motilal pioneered passive investing in India with their first NASDAQ 100 fund and followed with midcap products. Nifty Midcap 100 includes companies across diverse sectors — chemicals, textiles, FMCG, healthcare, industrials, IT — providing balanced exposure to India's growth engine. The index is more volatile than Nifty 50 (beta ~1.3) with higher drawdown risk during corrections (-40% to -50% in bear markets). However, the recovery is equally sharp — post-2020 COVID crash, midcap 100 delivered 120%+ returns in 18 months. Best suited for investors with 7+ year horizon willing to endure volatility for superior compounding. SIP suits midcap ETFs better than lump sum owing to volatility.
